title: 10x Thinking
html:
10x Thinking

Masayoshi Son's signature scaling rule: "Think bigger, 10x bigger." Captured in the Feb 2022 distillation and carried as recurring vocabulary across Yahir's ambition-language for the next four years — appears in college essays, OSU app drafts, and product framings well after the Son note.

Chronology

  • 2019-06-26 — 2019 06 26 goal and time frame — earliest precursor (HS-era goal-setting)
  • 2019-07-22 — 2019 07 22 run in morning — proto-discipline frame
  • 2019-08-06 — 2019 08 06 read books wireless charging brain waves — already speaking in scale-jump terms
  • 2019-11-10 — 2019 11 10 humor is the way to girls — tangential aphorism collection
  • 2020-03-06 — 2020 03 06 short story — narrative-form rehearsal
  • 2020-12-17 — 2020 12 17 osu app final — the OSU application uses 10x framing
  • 2021-11-05 — 2021 11 05 do you think that ceos of the future will be at a disadvanta — meta-question about future CEOs
  • 2022-02-28 — 2022 02 28 masayoshi son — canonical source — the "think bigger, 10x bigger" line lands here

Why it matters

10x-thinking is the lexical fingerprint of Yahir-the-ambitious-operator. It's not just a Son aphorism — once Yahir picks it up in Feb 2022, the language of "10x" becomes a tell across his project framings (Austin thesis, Outpace PRD ambition framing, Brotein "this could be the SaaS-for-fitness" framing). When he uses "10x", he's invoking Son's permission-to-think-bigger filter — even when Son isn't named.

Connections

masayoshi son · the austin thesis · outpace · brotein · ambition · no runway discipline
